summary: Bilateral filter (Tomasi & Manduchi): edge-preserving weighted average with a spatial Gaussian (spatial_sigma) times a range Gaussian (range_sigma) over the joint per-channel intensity distance; exact direct reference.

inputs.name: input
inputs.doc: The Image or Field1 to filter (range distance is the joint per-channel intensity difference).

outputs.name: output
outputs.doc: The bilaterally-filtered result (same kind and extent as the input).

params.name: spatial_sigma
params.doc: The spatial Gaussian standard deviation in pixels (window radius is ceil(3*spatial_sigma)); strictly positive.

params.name: range_sigma
params.doc: The range (intensity) Gaussian standard deviation; smaller values preserve sharper edges. Strictly positive.

perceptual.reason: filter.bilateral is a closed-form edge-preserving weighted average verified by analytic properties (constant-image identity, flat-region smoothing, step-edge preservation, the large-range-sigma Gaussian limit, the weighted average against an independent direct reference); there is no perceptual-quality metric to apply
